2606.10833.title: Do VLMs Reason Like Engineers? A Benchmark and a Stage-wise Evaluation
2606.10833.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) demonstrate strong performance on general multimodal reasoning benchmarks, yet their ability to perform engineering reasoning remains largely unexplored. Unlike general visual question answering, engineering problem solving requires interpreting technical diagrams, selecting governing physical principles, and maintaining physically consistent multi-step reasoning. These capabilities are increasingly important for AI systems used in engineering education, scientific assistance, and technical decision-making, where reasoning failures may produce physically invalid yet superficially plausible solutions. Existing benchmarks primarily evaluate final answers and provide limited assessment of intermediate reasoning processes. We introduce EngVQA, a multimodal benchmark for evaluating engineering reasoning across 5 engineering subjects containing 696 problems. We introduce an 8-stage automatic evaluation framework for assessing VLM-generated solutions. The framework independently evaluates each stage of the solution, enabling fine-grained analysis of reasoning failures. We benchmark multiple state-of-the-art open and closed source VLMs on our evaluation framework and demonstrate substantial limitations in current engineering reasoning capabilities. Human evaluation shows strong agreement with our automated framework, achieving a Pearson correlation of 0.975 and a mean absolute error of 0.67 on a 10-point grading scale. Our results highlight the importance of process-oriented evaluation for reliable assessment of multimodal engineering reasoning systems.
2606.10833.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在一般多模態推理基準上表現出色，但它們在工程推理方面的能力仍然大多未被探索。與一般的視覺問題回答不同，工程問題解決需要解釋技術圖表、選擇主導的物理原則，並保持物理上一致的多步推理。這些能力對於用於工程教育、科學輔助和技術決策的人工智慧系統越來越重要，因為推理失誤可能會產生物理上無效但表面上似乎合理的解決方案。現有的基準主要評估最終答案，對中間推理過程的評估有限。我們引入了EngVQA，一個針對5個工程科目、包含696個問題的多模態基準，用於評估工程推理。我們引入了一個8階段的自動評估框架，用於評估VLM生成的解決方案。該框架獨立評估解決方案的每個階段，從而使推理失誤的細緻分析成為可能。我們在我們的評估框架上對多個最先進的開源和閉源VLM進行基準測試，並展示了當前工程推理能力的重大限制。人類評估顯示與我們的自動化框架有強烈的一致性，在10分制的評分標準下達到0.975的皮爾森相關性和0.67的平均絕對誤差。我們的結果強調了以過程為導向的評估對於可靠評估多模態工程推理系統的重要性。
2606.10833.author: Syed Wasiq et.al.
2606.10833.authors: Syed Wasiq, Syed Mohamad Tawseeq, Yashwant Pravinrao Bangde, Debaditya Roy
